# Document trustProxyIdentity threat model + operator config in SharedDeployment.md

## Context

Retroactive ticket for the doc-work that should have been filed BEFORE [PR #10769](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10769) opened. The doc PR shipped in tight coordination with [PR #10768](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10768) (the code-substrate it documents) but I skipped the `ticket-create` step under self-imposed time pressure ‚Äî workflow discipline lapse.

## The Problem

[PR #10768](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10768) (`feat(auth): add proxy identity injection via X-PREFERRED-USERNAME`) ships the trust-boundary code for `auth.trustProxyIdentity` config + proxy-header reading + OIDC-precedence guard + source-tag propagation. The code is correct, but operators deploying the partner-trial topology need explicit documentation of the **load-bearing operational prerequisite**: the fronting proxy MUST strip client-set values of `X-PREFERRED-USERNAME` (and oauth2-proxy variants) before forwarding upstream, set them itself based on its own validated auth state, and prevent direct MC ingress.

Without this doc, operators could enable `trustProxyIdentity=true` without understanding the proxy-side prerequisite ‚Äî silent security hole under any default-allow-headers reverse proxy.

## The Architectural Reality

- `learn/agentos/SharedDeployment.md` ‚Äî the operator-facing deployment profile; auth section was missing
- PR #10768 changes: `auth.trustProxyIdentity` config field + `TransportService.mjs` proxy-header reading branch
- `Server.mjs#buildRequestContext:259` ‚Äî propagates `source: 'oidc' | 'proxy-header'` into the request context

## The Fix

Add an `## Authentication` section to `SharedDeployment.md` between `## Configuration` and `## Healthcheck Verification`. Cover:
- OIDC vs proxy-identity injection paths
- `trustProxyIdentity` env-var + config substrate
- The 3 proxy prerequisites (strip client-set headers; set them itself based on validated auth; prevent direct MC ingress)
- Both header conventions checked (`x-preferred-username` canonical + `x-auth-request-preferred-username` oauth2-proxy variant)
- Source-tag observability (`source: 'oidc'` vs `'proxy-header'` vs empty for local-dev)
- Fallback discipline: when any of the 3 proxy prerequisites is uncertain, leave `trustProxyIdentity=false`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `## Authentication` section added to `SharedDeployment.md`
- [ ] Threat-model statement explicit (3 proxy prerequisites)
- [ ] Both header conventions documented
- [ ] Source-tag observability documented
- [ ] OIDC precedence semantics explained
- [ ] Pairs structurally with embedding-provider documentation (parallel-track shape)

## Out of Scope

- Symmetric healthcheck `providers.auth` block (separate follow-up #10770)
- Unit tests for proxy-identity injection (separate follow-up #10772)
- Full OIDC-issuer provisioning steps (operator-side concern; out of MVP scope)

## Related

- **Code PR:** #10768 (`Resolves #10727` ‚Äî the trust-boundary code substrate)
- **Doc PR:** #10769 (resolves THIS ticket ‚Äî already merged 2026-05-05, retroactively-filed ticket)
- **Sibling parallel-track:** PR #10767 (`feat(memory-core): surface active embedding provider in healthcheck (#10723)`)
- **Parent epic:** #10721 (Shared deployment MVP completeness gaps)

**Note:** This ticket is filed RETROACTIVELY post-merge of PR #10769. Filed for graph-linkage cleanliness ‚Äî the doc work was real but the ticket-then-PR ordering was skipped under self-imposed time pressure (per `pull-request-workflow ¬ß0` discipline lapse). @tobiu calibration 2026-05-05: workflow-discipline > velocity even under operator-stated importance contexts.
